evals.prompt: Create a module called `project_tracker` for a Drupal 10 site that provides a content entity type called `project_item` with bundle support. Different project types (task, milestone, deliverable) should be separate bundles. Define a companion config entity type called `project_item_type` that serves as the bundle entity for project_item. The content entity should have base fields for: title (string), status (list_string with allowed values: open, in_progress, closed), and deadline (timestamp). Wire the bundle relationship properly between the content entity and config entity via annotations. Include all necessary handlers: forms, list builder, access control handler, and route provider. Provide admin routes for managing both the entity types and their instances. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: Content entity with bundle_entity_type, config entity with bundle_of, EntityChangedTrait with changed field, complete handler wiring
expectations: Content entity annotation/attribute includes bundle_entity_type key pointing to the config entity type ID (e.g., 'project_item_type') -- without this key Drupal does not know which config entity provides bundles for this content entity

Config entity annotation/attribute includes bundle_of key pointing to the content entity type ID (e.g., 'project_item') -- this creates the reverse relationship and without it the config entity is not recognized as a bundle provider
Content entity has 'bundle' key in entity_keys annotation mapping to a field (without this Drupal cannot determine which bundle an entity instance belongs to, causing fatal errors on entity creation)

Config entity class exists as a separate file from the content entity class -- one entity per file, config entity extends ConfigEntityBase or ConfigEntityBundleBase (NOT mixing content and config entity definitions in one class file)

Content entity uses EntityChangedTrait AND defines a 'changed' base field in baseFieldDefinitions() (the trait provides getChangedTime/setChangedTime methods but requires the base field to exist in the database -- having the trait without the field or the field without the trait is an incomplete pattern)

Content entity baseFieldDefinitions() calls parent::baseFieldDefinitions($entity_type) and uses the result (MUST call parent or core base fields like id, uuid, and langcode are lost, causing entity creation to fail with missing primary key errors)
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en project_tracker returns exit code 0

Log in as admin via drush uli, navigate to the project item type admin page (e.g., /admin/structure/project_item_type or similar collection route) -- verify the bundle management UI loads without errors (via eval-browser)
Generated module code passes phpcs --standard=Drupal,DrupalPractice static analysis without errors
